commitments.text: reduce global methane emissions by 30% by 2030 relative to 2020 levels

measurability.reason: The commitment has a specific number, unit, target year, and baseline.

gap_findings.description: Saudi Arabia's NDC commitment of 30% methane reduction by 2030 is lower than the IPCC recommended 34% reduction.
gap_findings.ndc_evidence: Reduce global methane emissions by 30% by 2030 relative to 2020 levels [NDC2_2021_Saudi_Arabia, p.7].
gap_findings.ipcc_evidence: Reducing methane ~34% by 2030 vs 2020 buys near-term warming relief and is cost-effective [WG3_Chapter04_MitigationDevelopmentPathways_2022, p.32].

gap_findings.description: Saudi Arabia's NDC commitment is conditional on receiving international finance.
gap_findings.ipcc_evidence: No mention of conditional dependency in IPCC AR6.

details.summary: Saudi Arabia's NDC commitment for methane reduction is lower than the IPCC recommended target, and it is conditional on receiving international finance.

pass_c.country_summary: Saudi Arabia's NDC commitment for methane reduction is lower than the IPCC recommended target, and it is conditional on receiving international finance.
pass_c.policy_recommendation: Saudi Arabia should strengthen its NDC commitment to align with the IPCC recommended 34% methane reduction by 2030 and remove the conditional dependency on international finance.
